title: MHI Successfully Produces Hydrogen at Its Ammonia Cracking Pilot Plant Using Steam Heating
content:
Mitsubishi Heavy Industries (MHI) has successfully produced 99% pure hydrogen at its Nagasaki pilot plant by cracking ammonia using steam heating. This achievement marks a world first for pilot-scale hydrogen production using this specific steam-based method. Unlike conventional technologies that rely on burner combustion, MHIâ€™s system operates at lower reaction temperatures, which reduces operating costs. The absence of a combustion furnace also allows for significant system miniaturization.

This technology supports the use of ammonia as an efficient "hydrogen carrier" for safe, long-distance transport. MHI aims to utilize this breakthrough to develop medium-scale, decentralized ammonia cracking systems located near hydrogen demand sites. The project is being accelerated in collaboration with partners Nippon Shokubai Co., Ltd. and Hokkaido Electric Power Co., Inc., and was selected by Japan's NEDO for its competitive hydrogen supply chain development initiative, contributing to global decarbonization efforts.
author: Analysts Account
